summary: Completed testing of Inventory Stock Tracking and Shopping List Integration features. All 15 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI testing confirmed all new features working correctly: Current Stock counter with +/- buttons, dynamic Stock Level badge calculation, Mark as Purchased workflow with expiry date picker.
passed_tests: Backend: Create inventory item with current_stock field
Backend: Update current_stock increase via API
Backend: Update current_stock decrease via API (min 0)
Backend: Stock level 'empty' when current_stock is 0
Backend: Stock level 'low' when current_stock is 1-25% of monthly need
Backend: Stock level 'half' when current_stock is 26-75% of monthly need
Backend: Stock level 'full' when current_stock is >75% of monthly need
Backend: Create shopping item with monthly_quantity
Backend: Shopping item stores monthly_quantity for purchase workflow
Backend: Mark as purchased creates new inventory item
Backend: Mark as purchased updates existing inventory current_stock
Backend: Mark as purchased with expiry date updates inventory
Backend: Delete shopping item after purchase
Backend: Inventory model has current_stock field in response
Backend: Update both current_stock and stock_level together
Frontend: Inventory page shows 'Current Stock' section with +/- buttons
Frontend: Inventory page shows 'Monthly Need' section with +/- buttons
Frontend: Stock Level badge is read-only and dynamically displayed
Frontend: Stock summary cards show Full/Half/Low/Empty counts
Frontend: Shopping page shows 'Purchased' button for each item
Frontend: Shopping page shows date picker for expiry date
Frontend: Mark as Purchased removes item from shopping list
Frontend: Mark as Purchased adds item to inventory with correct stock
Frontend: Mark as Purchased with expiry date shows 'Expires in X days'
Frontend: Toast notification confirms 'Added [item] to inventory'
critical_code_review_comments: The current_stock field is stored in base units (grams/ml) which is good for consistency
Stock level calculation logic (0%=empty, 1-25%=low, 26-75%=half, >75%=full) is correctly implemented in frontend
The handleMarkAsPurchased function correctly parses quantity strings and converts to base units
Existing inventory items without current_stock field will show 0 - this is expected behavior for legacy data
seed_data_creation: Created test inventory items (TEST_ prefixed) during backend tests - all cleaned up after tests. Frontend test marked 'Sugar' as purchased which added it to inventory.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All inventory stock tracking features verified: 1) Current Stock counter replaces dot toggles with +/- buttons, 2) Stock Level badge is now read-only and auto-calculated based on current_stock/monthly_quantity ratio, 3) Shopping page has 'Purchased' button and expiry date picker for each item, 4) Mark as Purchased workflow: adds quantity to inventory current_stock, updates expiry if provided, removes from shopping list. Test user: recipe_tester@test.com / TestPass123!

current_stock_counter.details: Inventory cards show 'Current Stock' section with - and + buttons. Clicking + increases stock by category step (e.g., 500g for grains). Stock value displayed in formatted units (e.g., '500 g', '1 kg').

dynamic_stock_level_badge.details: Stock Level badge is read-only, shows Empty/Low/Half/Full based on current_stock/monthly_quantity ratio. Colors: Empty=gray, Low=orange, Half=yellow, Full=green.

shopping_expiry_date_picker.details: Each shopping item has a date input (mm/dd/yyyy format) with calendar icon. Date is optional and used when marking as purchased.

mark_as_purchased_button.details: Green 'Purchased' button with checkmark icon. Clicking triggers: 1) Find/create inventory item, 2) Add quantity to current_stock, 3) Update expiry if provided, 4) Remove from shopping list, 5) Show success toast.

inventory_shopping_integration.details: Full workflow verified: Set expiry date -> Click Purchased -> Item removed from shopping (77->76 items) -> Item added to inventory with Full stock level -> Expiry shows 'Expires in 35 days'.
